TWEET 1
The AI model race just fragmented. The smartest executive move right now is not picking a winner — it is building for optionality.

---

TWEET 2
GPT-5.4 launched March 5 with 1M token context and Thinking/Pro variants. Anthropic shipped Claude Sonnet 4.6 in February for coding and agentic workloads.

Each model is optimizing for different lanes.

---

TWEET 3
Meta delayed its frontier model "Avocado" from March to May. Performance did not match Google or OpenAI despite massive infrastructure.

The moat is not compute alone. It is architecture and iteration speed.

---

TWEET 4
The strategic shift: AI procurement is no longer about choosing THE best model. It is about building systems that route each workload to the right model for the job.

---

TWEET 5
Enterprises locked into a single model vendor today are building the same risk companies built when they went all-in on one cloud provider in 2012.

Model-agnostic architecture is the new baseline.

---

TWEET 6

## Fact-check notes
- GPT-5.4 launched March 5, 2026, with GPT-5.4 Thinking and Pro variants and 1 million token context window (Source: UniFuncs, Global AI Industry Highlights March 7-8, 2026)
- Meta is reportedly delaying its frontier model 'Avocado' from March to May due to performance not matching Google and OpenAI models (Source: Stock Market Nerd, News of the Week March 9-13, 2026)
- Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 on February 4-5, 2026, with Sonnet 4.6 positioned for coding, agentic applications, and professional work at scale (Source: UniFuncs, Global AI Industry Recap March 9, 2026)
